origin.capability_match: Delegate $BGT to validators to boost their rewards

attack_surface.data_description: $BGT delegation transaction exploiting an accounting flaw (Note: DF10 is for NFTs but represents a user-initiated flow to Component 2)

security_gap.gap_description: Flaw in delegation logic for $BGT balance checking or influence calculation.

actor_risk.description: $BGT Holder/Delegator attempting to delegate more $BGT than owned.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Exploiting delegation logic can unfairly influence validator rewards and PoL.

assumptions: The smart contract in Berachain Core L1 (Component 2) for $BGT delegation has errors in balance checking or how delegation weight is applied.
An attacker can manipulate these errors to delegate $BGT (Asset 2) they don't own or gain unearned influence over validator rewards.

mitigation.strategy: Rigorous auditing of the delegation smart contract, ensuring correct balance updates, ownership checks, and fair application of delegated $BGT weight.

description: An attacker exploits a flaw in Berachain Core L1's (Component 2) delegation system to delegate $BGT (Asset 2) they do not own or to amplify the effect of their delegation beyond their actual stake, unfairly influencing validator boost calculations.

trace_source.matched_capability: Delegate $BGT to validators to boost their rewards
